description: Play/reset control for variableanimator or shape animator expressions. When display() is provided, renders a clickable play button inline next to the animator control. Without display(), renders a mutator pencil icon.
utility: Creates a play control that triggers the referenced animator's animation

arguments.name: animatorRef
arguments.description: Variable reference to a variableanimator or shape animator expression

arguments.name: duration
arguments.description: Animation duration using t() syntax, e.g. t(3) for 3 seconds

arguments.name: display
arguments.description: Button text. When provided, renders a clickable play button next to the animator instead of a pencil icon. Second string shown while playing.

alternates.description: Mutator pencil icon next to the animator control
Clickable play button inline next to the animator. Preferred for interactive lessons.

examples.scenario: Correct ordering: variableanimator, then dependents, then playanimator last
examples.expression:
var_anim_1 = variableanimator(at(2,10), "q_1", 2, 0.8)
q1 = point(graph_1, q1x, f(q1x))
line_2 = line(graph_1, p1, q1, c(red))
play_1 = playanimator(var_anim_1, t(5))

examples.scenario: Play button with display label — placed after all t-dependent shapes
examples.expression:
varanim_t = variableanimator(at(2, 30), "t", 0, 360)
P = anglepoint(G, uc, t, c(crimson), s(5))
radius_line = line(G, O, P, type(segment), c(gray))
play_anim = playanimator(varanim_t, t(8), display("Play", "Playing..."))

examples.scenario: Play variable animator for circle tangent rotation
examples.expression:
mathexpr_1 = variableanimator(at(3.7, 4.4), "a", 0, 360)
circle_1 = circle(graph_1, point(graph_1, 0, 0), radius(2))
tangent_1 = tangent(graph_1, circle_1, a)
playanim_1 = playanimator(mathexpr_1, t(3))

examples.scenario: Unit circle animation with play button, angle, tracetable
examples.expression:
varanim_t = variableanimator(at(2, 30), "t", 0, 360)
G = g2d(at(4, 1), size(20), range(-2, 2), type(geometry), grid())
O = point(G, 0, 0, s(3))
uc = circle(G, O, radius(1), c(steelblue), s(2))
P = anglepoint(G, uc, t, c(crimson), s(5))
radius_line = line(G, O, P, type(segment), c(gray))
xaxis_pt = point(G, 1, 0, defer())
angle_1 = angle(G, O, xaxis_pt, P, arcsize(35), arrowhead(start), c(orange), s(3))
cos_line = line(G, P, point(G, cos(rad(t)), 0, defer()), type(segment), c(dodgerblue), s(2))
sin_line = line(G, P, point(G, 0, sin(rad(t)), defer()), type(segment), c(tomato), s(2))
cos_pt = point(G, cos(rad(t)), 0, c(dodgerblue), s(4))
sin_pt = point(G, 0, sin(rad(t)), c(tomato), s(4))
trace_1 = tracepoint(G, P)
tt = tracetable(at(4, 24), slidingwindow(10), field(P, 'x', "cos theta"), field(P, 'y', "sin theta"), prec(3))
play_anim = playanimator(varanim_t, t(8), display("Play", "Playing..."))

notes: ORDERING RULE: playanimator() MUST be placed AFTER all expressions that reference the animated variable. Define variableanimator first, then all shapes/labels/expressions that use the variable, then playanimator last. Placing it before dependent expressions means the animation triggers before those shapes exist.
When display() is provided, a clickable play button appears inline next to the animator control — no at() needed.
Without display(), a mutator pencil icon appears next to the animator (legacy behavior).
Prefer display() for interactive lessons where users click to play.
